observations.recommendation:
1.16 We recommended that the Municipality continuously implement programs,
projects and activities that will aid and protect its constituents from the adverse
effects of pandemic.

1.17 Further, we reminded that Management advise the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to comply with the posting requirements of procurement related
to COVID-19 as requisite by GPPB Circular No. 02-2021 dated March 25, 2021 and
RA No. 11525 or the Vaccination Program Act of 2021.

We recommended and the management agreed to (a) Allocate fund for the expenses to secure copies of the title of the declared real properties and assign a focal person to who will focus on the requirements needed to enable the immediate transfer of title of the land under the name of the Municipality
observations.managementAction: (b) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to request and assign personnel that will prepare and maintain updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report
observations.reason:
According to the Municipal Assessor, no budget was allotted for the transfer of title of the said lots.
Interview with the Acting Municipal Treasurer revealed that due to lack of personnel in the MTO, the maintenance of PCs and PAR were not prioritized.
According to the Municipal Accountant, they will follow the procedures on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pertaining to the unserviceable and destroyed PPEs that will be scheduled in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management provides additional personnel to address the lapses/errors in collecting and recording RPT/SET Receivables and assist the Treasurer in the preparation of Certified List of Taxpayers to be submitted to the Accountant.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) come up with procedures for refunds or offsetting for the accounts with double/triple payments; (b) xxx; and (c) to strengthen internal control in the collections and recording of RPT/SET receivables to prevent lapses/errors committed and ensure the collection of all taxes due to avoid Notice of Charge.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant set up the RPT/SET Receivables at 100% in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I and the Municipal Assessor to complete the tax mapping in CY 2022.
observations.managementAction:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer requested additional manpower who will focus on posting of real property tax payments in the RPTARs.
Weekly RPT/SET reconciliation was performed to ensure that the correct amount was reflected on the records.
The Acting Municipal Treasurer instructed the collectors to treat the overpayments as advance payment and remind the taxpayers who had deficient taxes to settle their accounts.
The Acting Municipal Treasurer instructed person in charge of preparing Statement of Account to verify/check all properties to be paid by taxpayer.
According to the Municipal Accountant, the assessment roll did not provide information if the taxable property is indeed taxable or exempt from real property tax. Thus, the former Municipal Accountant understated the set-up of RPT/SET receivable due to inclusion of tax-exempt properties in the assessment roll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Local Development Council adhere with the guidelines set in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017 – 1 on the optimal utilization of the 20% Internal Revenue Allotment to contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic developmental and environmental management outcomes.
observations.managementAction:
The audit team was able to inquire on the reasons behind the delay in the project implementation which according to management were primarily due to the following causes:
a.) There were failure of bidding in the four projects bidded in CY 2022.
b.) Change in administration effective July 1, 2022; and
c.) There were also changes in the BAC composition.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to (a) ensure completeness of the supporting documents before payment of claims.
observations.managementAction: The LCE together with the MPDO, MEO and other offices concerned will provide a definite timeline for the PPAs. Also, the Management will organize a Local Project Monitoring Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer and the Municipal Engineering Office monitor and evaluate the report provided by the Municipal Accounting Office on the utilization/implementation of the PPAs incorporated in the 70% Mitigation Fund in order to achieve higher accomplishment /utilization rate.
observations.managementAction: The Management was continuously monitoring the utilization rate through utilization report provided by the Municipal Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the GAD focal person 1) submit the annual GAD Accomplishment Report with the (i) brief summary of the reported program or project; (ii) copies of reported policy issuances; (iii) results of HGDG tests, and (iv) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations; 2) fully implement the GAD activities identified in their GPB and complete the establishment of the CBMS to facilitate and develop the Municipality’s GAD Database; and 3) create/assign a Responsibility Center (RC) for their GFPS to facilitate generation of all reports pertaining to GAD-related expenses and other GAD-related financial transactions, and to monitor and properly account for the GAD Funds in compliance with COA Circular 2021-008.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD accomplishment rate of the Municipality increased from 21.80% for CY 2021 to 45.87% this CY 2022.
According to the GAD Focal Person, for CY 2022, the report of the CBMS was not yet final and summarized by the Management Information System (MIS) Department.
According to the Municipal Accountant, assignment of responsibility center for the GFPS for GAD-related expenses was not yet established in the Accounting Department. She commented that they only mark the GAD related expenses in the excel file reports and create a separate file summarizing all the GAD expenditures. She added that they will assign a RC this year to monitor and properly account the GAD Funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that 1) management require the drivers/passengers requesting fuel/gasoline to prepare and properly fill out trip tickets and the office of the MPDO to prepare summary/monthly report of fuel consumption
observations.managementAction: 2) adequate control procedures be adopted to monitor and regulate fuel consumption

observations.reason:
Verification of the land account based on the inventory of land holdings submitted by the inventory committee as of December 2020 disclosed that 34 parcels of recorded land were not yet titled under the Municipality, thus validity of ownership could not be determined.
Though, there was already a personnel assigned to maintain Property Cards (PCs) per category of PPE owned by the municipality, PCs were not maintained because the documents such as Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) which contain the basic information necessary in maintaining the PCs were not available.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management (a) require the drivers/passengers requesting fuel/gasoline to prepare and properly fill up trip tickets; and
(b) the Office of the MPDO and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare summary/monthly report of fuel consumption in adherence with Section 361 of GAAM, Volume 1.
We also recommended that adequate control procedures be adopted to monitor and regulate fuel consumption.
observations.reason: Comparable with the results of post-audit and examination of supporting documents in last year's audit, Issue Slip (Driver’s Trip Ticket) attached to the disbursement vouchers still were not serially numbered and most of the time, places visited were not specific and purpose of the trips was not specified. The type of vehicle and vehicle plate number were not also indicated and most of the attached electronic receipts are faded, precluding us from verifying the legitimacy of the travel and the fuel charged against the government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (a) complete the establishment of the CBMS to facilitate and develop the Municipality’s GAD Database; and
observations.reason: Currently, only 80.78% had been completed because of lockdown and skeletal workforce due to the outbreak of COVID-19. The database was not yet fully established to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

observations.recommendation:
1.34 We recommended that the Municipality continue its efforts to further assess
and provide solutions to the effects of the pandemic.
1.35 We also recommended and the Management agreed to continue its adherence
with various guidelines prescribed by the different national government agencies
and other regulating agencies in COVID-19 measures and fund utilization.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer request additional personnel to assist her in the preparation of Certified List of Taxpayers based on the updated Assessment Roll to be submitted to the Accountant; and
(b) Municipal Accountant set up the RPT/SET Receivables at 100% and continue the maintenance of the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: Previously, the Audit Team was informed that current values were already encoded in the system and the treasury department already provided the accounting department with partial lists of paid taxpayers and property. However, interview with the new Municipal Treasurer disclosed that PATAS, an RPT networking software initiated by Provincial Government of Bataan was no longer in use since the linkage was not established between the Treasury and the Assessor’s Office, hence, the preparation and updating of the certified list of taxpayers identifying the names of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible was not completed. It was added further that the department needed additional manpower since the two personnel assigned in RPT collection were already in charge of the computation, receiving and posting of the payment, hence the posting/computation should be delegated to another personnel.
observations.reason:
Interview with the new Municipal Treasurer disclosed that PATAS,  was no longer in use since the linkage was not established between the Treasury and the Assessor’s Office, hence, the preparation and updating of the certified list of taxpayers identifying the names of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible was not completed.
The RPT/SET Receivables was set up at the beginning of the year totaling ₱62,721,426.00 which is 85% of ₱73,789,911, the total amount of tax due and collectibles from the assessment roll.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, if feasible, assigned/appoint a Market Administrator who will handle economic enterprises particularly the market and slaughterhouse and will focus in adopting measures and strategies to remedy the low collection of income from economic enterprises and minimize the expenses in relation thereto in order to generate sustainable revenues.
We further recommended that Management adopt policies that will maximize the benefits/use of the newly constructed fish landing facility.
observations.reason: A revenue code has yet to be passed for the operation of the fish landing facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer ensure completeness of the supporting documents before payment of claims.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the documentary requirements for the procurement of civil works were already complied by management and we appreciate the effort and hard work to comply with some of the deficiencies noted by the audit team in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that Management:
(a) through the Solid Waste Management Board, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the municipality including the MOA on Materials Recovery facility and take appropriate action, if possible and feasible, to established their own MRF; and
(b) ensure full implementation of the PPA’s under 20% development fund that were not implemented in CY 2020 to continuously develop the eco waste facility.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2020, an information education campaign seminar was conducted by management for the barangays to allot funds for the establishments of MRF in the Barangays.
observations.reason: For the continuous development of eco waste facility, the purchase of dumptruck is already on process, while management is currently looking for another supplier for the upgrade of machinery/conveyor to be use in the centralized Materials Recovery Facility.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended that  LDRRM Officers
(a) submit the lacking documents in the procurement of the goods and equipment used in containing the spread of COVID-19.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the:

(a) Management (i) allocate fund for the expenses to secure copies of the title of the declared real properties; and

(b)Municipal Treasurer, Assessor and Accountant exert effort to verify the existence of the title of the declared real properties and secure copies thereof.
observations.managementAction: There was already an updated inventory of land holdings owned by the municipality.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Verification of the land account based on the inventory of land holdings submitted by the inventory committee as of December 2020 disclosed that 34 parcels of recorded land were not yet titled under the Municipality, thus validity of ownership could not be determined.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management 

(a) appoint additional personnel and assigned  as a Market Administrator to handle economic enterprises particularly the market and slaughterhouse who will focus in adopting measures and strategies to remedy the low collection of income for economic enterprises and minimize the expenses in relation thereto to generate sustainable revenues.
observations.reason: There is no assigned yet as Market Administrator. The responsibility is still lodged at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: 1.12 We recommended that Management, in its future disbursement of funds, adhere to the provisions of (a) Section 116, Chapter 7, Volume 1 of New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Unit (LGU); (b) Section 4 of GPPB No.08-2009 Guidelines on Lease Venue; and (c) Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 as discussed above.

2.22 We commended Management and the MSWD for the effective system of profiling distribution and liquidation of the 1st Tranche of SAP in accordance with the Memorandum Circular. We also recommended that a representation be made to the DSWD RO III to follow up the payout of the beneficiaries included in the claim list who haven’t receive yet the second tranche of SAP.

2.23 We also recommended that Management in the future preparation of payrolls should not commit erasures/alterations in the lists but instead write/put in remarks that these were refunded/returned and prepare a new list of payrolls for the replacement so as not to cast doubts on the integrity of the liquidation documents.

3.18 We recommended that the:
(a) Inventory Committee led by the Internal Audit Service Department, if feasible, complete the inventory of buildings and structures owned by the Municipality and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to enable reconciliation with the accounting and property records and correction of verified discrepancies;
(b) Management allocate fund for the expenses to secure copies of the title of the declared real properties and assigned a focal person to who will focus on the requirements needed to enable the immediate transfer of title of the land under the name of the Municipality; and
(c) Municipal Treasurer request and assigned personnel tasked to prepare and maintain updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report.

4.11 We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to coordinate and follow up with the Provincial Government of Bataan for the immediate action in resolving the problems encountered in the PATAS;
(b) Municipal Treasurer request additional personnel to assist her in the preparation of Certified List of Taxpayers based on the updated Assessment Roll to be submitted to the Accountant; and
(c) Municipal Accountant set up the RPT/SET Receivables at 100% and continue the maintenance of the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies.

5.14 We recommended that Management, if feasible, assigned/appoint a Market Administrator who will handle economic enterprises particularly the market and slaughterhouse and will focus in adopting measures and strategies to remedy the low collection of income from economic enterprises and minimize the expenses in relation thereto in order to generate sustainable revenues.

5.15 We further recommended that Management adopt policies that will maximize the benefits/use of the newly constructed fish landing facility.

6.5 We recommended that Management require the a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer ensure completeness of the supporting documents before payment of claims; and b) submit to the Auditor’s Office the required documents specified in Appendix E in compliance with the above-cited circulars to facilitate review and verification thereof.

7.8 We recommended that Management require the drivers/passengers requesting fuel/gasoline to prepare and properly fill up trip tickets; and the Office of the MPDO and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare summary/monthly report of fuel consumption in adherence with Section 361 of GAAM, Volume 1.  We also recommended that adequate control procedures be adopted to monitor and regulate fuel consumption.

8.21 We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that Management:
through the Solid Waste Management Board, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the municipality including the MOA on Materials Recovery facility and the strict implementation of the newly enacted laws/ordinance in the LGU and take appropriate action, if possible and feasible, to established their own MRF;
ensure full implementation of the PPA’s under 20% development fund that were not implemented in CY 2020 to continuously develop the eco waste facility; and
c.) continue all its efforts and initiative in improving its waste diversion activities and in maintaining the operations of a Sanitary Landfill in accordance with Section 42 of the Act on the compliance with the minimum requirements for the operation of SLF.

9.5 We recommended that the Management (a) submit a copy of the Annual GPB to the COA Audit Team within five (5) workings days from the receipt of the approved plan and annual GAD Accomplishment Report complete with the required documents for proper evaluation thereof; (b) complete the establishment of the CBMS to facilitate and develop the Municipality’s GAD Database; and (c) reorganize the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) Team to include CSO representatives from the academe; and representatives of LCE for proper representation and evaluation of its GAD policies, programs and projects.

10.27 We recommended that the Management fully capacitate the mandatory position in LDRRMO particularly the operations and warning.

10.28 We also recommended that LDRRM Officers (a) submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF with the prescribed signatories; (b) observe the guidelines set by the higher authorities such as GPPB, DILG, DBM and COA in relation to provide measures in addressing the COVID-19 situation in the Municipality; and (c) submit the lacking documents in the procurement of the goods and equipment used in containing the spread of COVID-19.

We recommended and Management agreed that the: 

(a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to coordinate and follow up with the Provincial Government of Bataan for the immediate action in resolving the errors encountered in the PATAS in the Treasury Department; and
observations.managementAction: The linkage between the Assessor and the Provincial Government of Bataan was established and fully functioning.
observations.reason: the linkage between Assessor’s Office and the Municipal Treasury was not established, indicating a problem with PATAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Local Development Council to abide strictly with the guidelines on the planning and implementation of the projects/activities funded under the 20% Development Fund so as not to delay the desired development outcomes for its people.

We recommended that Management require the 

a.) Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirements and 




b) submit to the Auditor’s Office the required documents specified in Appendix A in compliance with Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, COA Circular No. 2012-001, Section 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 and Item Nos. 2 and 3 of DPWH Department Order No. 216 for all projects implemented by the Municipality to facilitate review and verification thereof.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the documentary requirements for the procurement of civil works were already complied by management and we appreciate the effort and hard work to comply with some of the deficiencies noted by the audit team in CY 2019.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Some deficiencies were still noted in the documentary requirements of the projects hence, review and verification thereof was not completed.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Management for their efforts which led to the approval of its ten-year solid waste management plan and in ensuring that the closure of the existing dumpsite was done in a proper manner, with environmental protection and safeguard in place. 

We also commended the Management in effectively implementing its centralized MRF and sanitary landfill in accordance with pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 9003.

Likewise, we recommended that the Management:

a)	through the Solid Waste Management Board, encourage active participation of Barangay Officials to support the solid waste management program of the Municipality and take appropriate action, if possible and feasible, to established their own MRF; 

b) ensure full implementation of the PPA’s under 20% development fund that were not implemented in CY 2019 to continuously develop the eco waste facility; and


c.) continue all its efforts and initiative in maintaining the operations of a Sanitary Landfill in accordance with Section 42 of the Act on the compliance with the minimum requirements for the operation of SLF.
observations.managementAction:
The nine (9) Barangays in absence of MRF’s,  have signed a Memorandum of Agreement with the accredited junkshop in their respective barangays called the Materials Recovery System


For the continuing appropriations for CY 2020, management were able to accomplished the construction of cell and construction of road for the continous Development of Eco Waste Facility. 


We have noted that management is continuously spearheading different projects for the sustainable development of the MRF and the Sanitary Landfill.
observations.reason:
The Nine (9) Barangays have yet to established their own MRF.

Partially Implemented

Several projects for the continuous development of Eco Waste Facility was allotted under the 20% DF,   however,  said project was not implemented during the year because of the pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management

(a) submit a copy of the Annual GPB to the COA Audit Team within five (5) workings days from the receipt of the approved plan and annual GAD Accomplishment Report complete with the required documents for proper evaluation thereof; 


(b) continue to established the CBMS to facilitate and develop the Municipality’s GAD Database; and 


(c) reorganize the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) Team to include CSO representatives from women’s organization, the academe and representatives of LCE for proper representation and evaluation of its GAD policies, programs and projects.
observations.reason:
The Annual GPB and Annual GAD Accomplishment Report was submitted on February 2, 2021. The Annual GPB was not submitted on time while the Annual GAD AR was submitted on time but no attached supporting documents.

Partially Implemented

The CBMS is still on-going and now on its 87% completion.

Partially Implemented

Although the GAD M&E Team was reorganized for CY 2020, nonetheless, it was still not in accordance with JMC No. 2013-01.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer for transferring the unexpended balance at year-end and the Municipal Budget Officer for the creation of LDRRMO III position. 


We also recommended that LDRRM Officers 
(a) submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF with the prescribed signatories.
observations.reason: Submitted on time however, not all of the monthly reports were duly certified by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to coordinate and follow up with
the Provincial Government of Bataan for the immediate action in resolving the
errors encountered in the PATAS in the Treasury Department; and
(b) Municipal Accountant to update and complete the corresponding subsidiary
ledgers (SLs) once the Assessor updated their assessment rolls based on the new tax
mapped real properties to monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance
with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.

2.5 We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the:
(a) Management
(i) assigned a focal person as additional member of the Inventory Committee
created that will initiate and focus on the conduct of the annual physical
inventory of PPE by type, with the end view of establishing the correctness of the
reported balances as well as the existence, condition and custodianship of the
municipal assets and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year
to enable reconciliation with the accounting and property records and correction
of verified discrepancies; and
(ii) allocate fund for the expenses to secure copies of the title of the declared real
properties;
(b) Municipal Treasurer
(i) request and assigned personnel that will prepare and maintain updated
property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the
inventory report;
(ii) coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to obtain copies of the tax
declarations of all properties, verify the inclusion of all land in the inventory list
against the tax declarations obtained and act on any discrepancy noted,
determine whether all lands where the buildings/facilities owned by the LGU are
built are titled to the Municipality; and
(iii) maintain a Summary List with costs of all the real properties;
(c) Municipal Accountant
(i) maintain updated PPELC; and
(ii) coordinate with the Treasury Department for proper recording of each
lot/land owned by the municipal government; and
(d) Municipal Treasurer, Assessor and Accountant exert effort to verify the
existence of the title of the declared real properties and secure copies thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed that the: 

(a) Municipal Treasurer follow up with the Provincial Government of Bataan for the immediate action in resolving the linkage problem of the PATAS; 

(b) Municipal Assessor expedite the tax mapping; and

(c) Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:

2018

Tax mapping of the Assessor’s Office is almost complete with only one barangay  left to encode in the system.

Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) in Excel format.

Partially Implemented


Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the:
(a) Management
(i) create an Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical inventory of PPE by type, with the end view of establishing the correctness of the reported balances as well as the existence, condition and custodianship of the municipal assets and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to enable reconciliation with the accounting and property records and correction of verified discrepancies; 

(ii) allocate fund for the expenses to secure copies of the title of the declared real properties; and 

(iii) create a Disposal Committee and comply with the proper procedure in the disposal of unserviceable properties.

(b) Municipal Treasurer:
(i) prepare and maintain updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report; 

(ii) obtain copies of the tax declarations of all properties, verify the inclusion of all land in the inventory list against the tax declarations obtained and act on any discrepancy noted, determine whether all lands where the buildings/facilities owned by the LGU are built are titled to the Municipality; and

(iii) maintain a Summary List with costs of all the real properties;

(c) Municipal Accountant: (i) maintain updated PPELC; (ii) coordinate with the Treasury Department for recording the distinctiveness of each lot/land owned by the municipal government; and

(d) Municipal Treasurer, Assessor and Accountant exert effort to verify the existence of the title of the declared real properties and secure copies thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Municipal Treasurer for transferring the unexpended balance at year-end. We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the filling-up of the LDRRMO Head in the MDRRMO to strengthen its function. 

We also recommended  and Management agreed that LDRRM Officers

(a) monitor the full implementation of the LDRRMP towards the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121; and

(b) utilize the yearly 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF for the programs, project and activities identified for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 

Local Chief Executive 

(a) fully implement waste segregation at source and extend assistance to the barangays to establish their own MRF;

observations.managementAction: The Management will implement waste segregation at source and extend assistance to the barangays to establish their own MRF; and re-submitted their revised 10-year SWMP for approval.

observations.recommendation: 1.9	We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Treasurer follow up with the Provincial Government of Bataan for the immediate action in resolving the linkage problem of the PATAS; (b) Municipal Assessor expedite the tax mapping; and (c) Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.

2.8  We recommended that Management require the (a) Municipal Treasurer to record only the issued checks in their report; and (b) Municipal Accountant to install controls to minimize the influx of transactions on the last day of the year by obligating the expenditures at the time goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received.

3.4   We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the:
(a) Management (i) create an Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical inventory of PPE by type, with the end view of establishing the correctness of the reported balances as well as the existence, condition and custodianship of the municipal assets and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to enable reconciliation with the accounting and property records and correction of verified discrepancies; (ii) allocate fund for the expenses to secure copies of the title of the declared real properties; and (iii) create a Disposal Committee and comply with the proper procedure in the disposal of unserviceable properties.
(b)  Municipal Treasurer (i) prepare and maintain updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report; (ii) obtain copies of the tax declarations of all properties, verify the inclusion of all the lands in the inventory list against the tax declarations obtained and act on any discrepancy noted, determine whether all lands where the buildings/facilities owned by the LGU are built are titled to the Municipality; and (iii) maintain a Summary List with costs of all the real properties;
(c)  Municipal Accountant (i) maintain updated PPELC; (ii) coordinate with the Treasury Department for recording the distinctiveness of each lot/land owned by the municipal government; and (iii) correct the erroneous  classification; and
(d) Municipal Treasurer, Assessor and Accountant exert effort to verify the existence of the title of the declared real properties and secure copies thereof.
4.7  We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to reverse the entry made in recording the other payables amounting to  ₱33,000,000.00.

5.7	We recommended and Management agreed to convert the open type Evacuation Center into a close type building, provide toilet for PWD, potable water supply, storage area, Health Station/Clinic Infirmary and DRRM/Camp Management desk or office to comply with the Technical and Financial Standards of Evacuation Facility Project under the Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities Menu of Programs. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Auditor’s office the various required documents for review and verification of the transactions.

6.7	We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents in compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-01 before payment and  submit to the Auditor’s Office the various required documents for the projects of the DOH Health Facilities Enhancement Program and SALINTUBIG to facilitate review and verification of the transactions.

7.7	We recommended and Management agreed to require the a) Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the supporting documents before payment; and b) Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirements and submit to the Auditor’s Office the set of approved plans and drawings, technical specifications,  log book, as built plans  and other required documents specified in Annex C in compliance with Section 17 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, Item Nos. 2 and 3 of DPWH Department Order No. 216, series of 2000 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for all projects implemented by the Municipality to facilitate review and verification thereof.

8.16	We commended the Municipal Treasurer for transferring the unexpended balance at year-end. We recommended and Management  agreed to prioritize the filling-up of the LDRRMO Head in the MDRRMO to strengthen its function.
8.17	We also recommended  and Management agreed that LDRRM Officers (a) monitor the full implementation of the LDRRMP towards the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121; and (b) utilize the yearly 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF for the programs, project and activities identified for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation.

9.5	We recommended  and Management agreed to  (a) strictly comply on the required composition of SWMB for proper representation thereof; (b) make representation with the EMB for the approval of the 10-year SWMP; (c) provide support for the 9 Barangays to established their own/clustered MRF or  at least composting areas to comply with the Act and ensure proper waste disposal within their respective area; (d) conduct IEC to fully educate the barangays on the proper waste segregation; and (e) strictly enforce the garbage collectors to wear their personal protective gears to ensure their safety and protection.

10.4	We recommended that the Municipality (a) submit a copy of the Annual GPB to the COA Audit Team within five (5) workings days from the receipt of the approved plan and GAD Accomplishment Report together with the required documents within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year for proper evaluation thereof; (b) reconcile the amount of utilization of accomplishments as per reports of the Municipal Accountant and GFPS-TWG Chairperson; (c) gather and establish sex-disaggregated data and incorporate in the GAD Database; and (d) create the  GAD Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) Team for proper evaluation of the results of the LGU’s GAD PPAs.

11.5	We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant submit the financial reports, supporting documents and other schedules to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by existing rules and regulations.

11.7	We also reiterated that the LCE augment the manpower needed by the accounting office to properly distribute the workload and assist the Municipal Accountant expedite the submission of the financial reports, supporting documents and other schedules.

12.4	We commended the Management’s effort to withhold and remit taxes in accordance with the regulations issued by the BIR. We recommended and Management agreed to remit the contributions within the prescribed period mandated by BIR.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:

(a)Local        (a) Local Chief Executive:

request the Provincial Government of Bataan that the offices of the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant be provided linkage to the PATAS;


 assign personnel to assist the Treasurer to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers to be submitted to the Accountant; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant:


take up in the books, the RPT/SET Receivables; and 



prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.

of PATAS .






The Municipal Accountant takes up in the books, the RPT/SET Receivables and prepares the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections.

The LDRRM Officers will monitor implementation of the LDRRMP; utilize Mitigation Fund for the PPAs identified for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation; and  submit utilization report of the LDRRM fund on time.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the:

 Local Chief Executive:
 b) augment the manpower needed by the accounting office to properly distribute the workload and help the Municipal Accountant expedite the submission of the financial reports, supporting documents and other schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) LCE ensure strict implementation of the ordinances issued for the operations and franchising of tricycles and pedicab for hire especially on proper checking and inspection of the tricycles for the minimum requirements prior to the issuance or renewal of the franchise; and (b) SB members revisit/review the municipal  ordinances already enacted for the franchising/operations of tricycles/pedicab units and issue other ordinances, if necessary, in compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2007-01.

observations.reason:
The Municipality  failed to fully implement the ordinances/guidelines issued.
 
Reiterated in Obs. No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accountant, in coordination with other Offices involved to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies, and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing and payment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was not able to  require the  submission  of MOOE Allocations of primary and secondary schools.
observations.reason:
Due to continuous charging of expenses which are not among the prioritized expenses.
 
Reiterated in Obs. No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) LCE request the Provincial Government of Bataan that the offices of the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant and LCE be provided linkage to the PATAS but meantime, assign personnel to assist the Treasurer to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers to be submitted to the Accountant; and (b) Accountant take up in the books, the RPT/SET Receivables and prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.

We recommended and Management agreed that the: a)Management (i) create an inventory committee to conduct an annual physical inventory of PPE by type, with the end view of establishing the correctness of the reported balances as well as the existence, condition and custodianship of the municipal assets and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to enable reconciliation with the accounting and property records and correction of verified discrepancies; and (ii) allocate fund for the expenses to secure copies of the title of the declared real properties; b) Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report, obtain copies of the tax declarations of all properties, verify the inclusion of all land in the inventory list against the tax declarations obtained and act on any discrepancy noted, determine whether all lands where the buildings/facilities owned by the LGU are built are titled to the Municipality and maintain a Summary List with costs of all the real properties; c) Municipal Accountant maintain updated PPELC and coordinate with the Treasury Department for recording the distinctiveness of each lot/land owned by the municipal government; and d) Municipal Treasurer, Assessor and Accountant exert effort to verify the existence of the title of the declared real properties and secure copies thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor’s Office the set of approved plans and drawings with technical specifications, PERT/CPM analysis, log book, as built plans and other required documents specified in Annex A in compliance with Section 17 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, Item Nos. 2 and 3 of DPWH Department Order No. 216, series of 2000 dated November 16, 2000 and Item Nos. 9.1, 9.1.1 and 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 5 of DOLE Department Order (DO) No. 13 for all projects implemented by the Municipality to avoid notice of suspension and disallowance.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Accountant, in coordination with other Offices involved, to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DV before processing and payment; (b) refrain from charging expenses which are not among the prioritized expenses and ensure that allowances, if granted, are with legal basis; (c) request all the  primary and secondary public schools  to submit their budget as per their MOOE Allocations; and (d) record the expenditures under the  appropriate specific accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to restore the intelligence fund incurred in 2010 and 2011 as advances to officers and employees for which no Credit Advice had been issued by the COA.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
BUB Project-DBM-NAPC- Integrated Community Food Production (ICFP)
a) request the BPSU to issue  AF 51 instead of the unofficial receipt with BPSU logo;
BUB Project-DSWD-Sustainable Livelihood Program-Micro Enterprise b) submit the proof of Instruction to Bidders, Notice of Award in the Philgeps and the accomplished/signed payroll; c) justification of the mode of procurement use; and d) submit to the Auditor’s Office the various required documents for the projects funded by the BUB-Project-DOLE- Integrated Employment Program (DILEEP) Kabuhayan Program, BUB Project- National Irrigation Administration RO III (NIA), BUB Project-DILG-Flood Control, DILG-SALINTUBIG Program and DBM/DILG- Aid to Disadvantage Municipalities (ADM)- Priority Projects.

We commended the Municipality’s effort to comply with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. We recommended that the Municipality (a) submit a copy of the Annual GPB to the COA Audit Team within five (5) workings days from the receipt of the approved plan and Accomplishment Report together with the required documents within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year; (b) reconcile the amount of utilization of accomplishments as per reports of the Municipal Accountant and GFPS-TWG Chairperson; (c) gather and process sex-disaggregated data and incorporate in the GAD Database; and (d) implement, monitor and evaluate the adopted GAD Code of the Province of Bataan in the Municipality.

We recommended that designated LDRRM Officers (a)monitor the full implementation of the LDRRMP towards the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121; (b) utilize the yearly 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF for the programs, project and activities identified for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation; (c) strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the preparation and submission of the utilization report of LDRRM Fund to the Auditor every 15th day of each month for timely review and verification: and (d) monitor the full implementation of LDRRMF plan towards the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121.

We commended the substantial efforts done by the Municipality by issuing the ordinances in 2017.  We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure:
strict implementation of the ordinances issued for the operations and franchising of tricycles and pedicabs for hire; and
inclusion in the checklist on proper checking and inspection of the tricycles the minimum requirements in the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2007-01 prior to the issuance or renewal of the franchise.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) fully implement waste segregation at source and extend assistance to the barangays to establish their own MRF; (b) make representation with the EMB for the approval of the 10-year SWMP; and (c) ensure immediate completion of the centralized MRF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant submit the financial reports, supporting documents and other schedules to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by existing rules and regulations to evade any disciplinary action that may be instituted against them.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the LCE augment the manpower needed by the accounting office to properly distribute the workload and help the Municipal Accountant expedite the submission of the financial reports, supporting documents and other schedules.
We recommended that Management comply with the observations communicated through audit observation memoranda.

We recommended that Management require the (a) Engineering Division to submit to the Auditor’s Office the set of approved plans and drawings with technical specifications and PERT/CPM analysis and other required documents specified in Annex A in compliance with Section 17 of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184, Item Nos. 2 and 3 of DPWH Department Order No. 216, series of 2000, Item Nos. 9.1, 9.1.1 and 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 5 of DOLE Department Order (DO) No. 13 for all projects implemented by the Municipality; (b) BAC to specify clearly the provision on warranty security in the Bidding Documents; and (c) Contractors to (i) maintain log book, prepare As Built Plans and submit them to the Auditor’s Office; and (ii) post warranty security for the infrastructure projects which were completed and finally accepted by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Eng'g Dept. partially comply & provide the necessary  documents required.
observations.reason:
Due to partial submission of the required documents.

Reiterated in Obs. No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Local Chief Executive fill the positions created in the LDRRMO who will comprise the governance framework for management of disaster risk reduction and mitigation activities; (b) designated LDRRM Officers monitor the full implementation of the LDRRMP towards the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121 and; (c) Acting LDRRMO, Accountant, Budget Officer and Treasurer strictly adhere to the policies and procedures on the preparation of LDRRMP, utilization of the LDRRM Fund and transfer the unutilized balance thereof to the Special Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Personnel Dept. filled up only two positions to be  LDRRM personnel.
observations.reason:
Due to lack of  personnel and other deficiencies noted.

Reiterated in Obs. No. 8

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) GFPS, TWG and all officials concerned attend relevant trainings on GAD to acquire technical know-how and continue their efforts in gathering knowledge and information to be utilized as guide in the formulation of GAD Plan as well as other reports required; (b) Chairman of the GFPS assist the Local Sanggunian in the formulation of GAD Code to support the LGU’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, women’s economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality; (c) Local Planning and Development Office spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and (d) reconcile the amount of utilization of accomplishments  as per reports of the Municipal Accountant and GFPS-TWG Chairperson.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Database  was already adopted however,  the management was not able  to arrange trainings of the concerned Officials  for GAD awareness, plan, policy & budget formulation to be  knowledgeable to gather information f formulation of GAD  Code.
observations.reason:
GAD Database is still in process and no seminars was attended by the concerned officials for the formulation  of the GAD Code.

Reiterated in Obs. No. 7

Based on this, the Accountant should take up in the books, the RPT/SET Receivables with the corresponding SL that will monitor tax collections and delinquencies.

100% of the PATAS software already downloaded although linkage to the Treasury, Accounting
and Mayor’s office were not yet installed.

We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Management (i) create an inventory committee to conduct an annual physical inventory of PPE by type, with the end view of establishing the correctness of the reported balances as well as the existence, condition and custodianship of the municipal assets and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to enable reconciliation with the accounting and property records and correction of verified discrepancies; and (ii) allocate fund for the expenses to secure copies of the title of the declared real properties; (b) Municipal Treasurer (i) prepare and maintain updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report; (ii) obtain copies of the tax declarations of all properties; (iii) verify the inclusion of all land in the inventory list against the tax declarations obtained and act on any discrepancy noted; (iv) determine whether all lands where the buildings/facilities owned by the LGU are built are titled to the Municipality; and (v) maintain a Summary List with costs of all the real properties; (c) Municipal Accountant maintain updated PPELC and coordinate with the Treasury Department for recording the distinctiveness of each lot/land owned by the municipal government; and (d) Municipal Treasurer, Assessor and Accountant exert effort to verify the existence of the title of the declared real properties and secure copies thereof.

We recommended that Management require the (a) Engineering Division to submit to the Auditor’s Office the set of approved plans and drawings with technical specifications and PERT/CPM analysis and other required documents specified in Annex A in compliance with Section 17 of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184, Item Nos. 2 and 3 of DPWH Department Order No. 216, series of 2000, Item Nos. 9.1, 9.1.1 and 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 5 of DOLE Department Order (DO) No. 13 for all projects implemented by the Municipality; (b) BAC to specify clearly the provision on warranty security in the Bidding Documents; and (c) Contractors to (i) maintain log book, prepare As Built Plans and submit them to the Auditor’s Office; and (ii) post warranty security for the infrastructure projects which were completed and finally accepted by the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Local Chief Executive fill the positions created in the LDRRMO who will comprise the governance framework for management of disaster risk reduction and mitigation activities; (b) designated LDRRM Officers monitor the full implementation of the LDRRMP towards the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121 and; (c) Acting LDRRMO, Accountant, Budget Officer and Treasurer strictly adhere to the policies and procedures on the preparation of LDRRMP, utilization of the LDRRM Fund and transfer the unutilized balance thereof to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that the (a) GFPS, TWG and all officials concerned attend relevant trainings on GAD to acquire technical know-how and continue their efforts in gathering knowledge and information to be utilized as guide in the formulation of GAD Plan as well as other reports required; (b) Chairman of the GFPS assist the Local Sanggunian in the formulation of GAD Code to support the LGU’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, women’s economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality; (c) Local Planning and Development Office spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and (d) reconcile the amount of utilization of accomplishments  as per reports of the Municipal Accountant and GFPS-TWG Chairperson.

We recommended (a) that the Municipal Treasurer regulate and monitor strictly the use of motor vehicles and fuel consumption by ensuring that all travels are covered by properly accomplished and duly approved driver’s trip tickets and requisition issue slips and that all Monthly Report of Official Travels and Reports of Fuel Consumption are submitted to the Auditor for review and verification; and (b) refrain from utilizing current year’s appropriation to pay prior year’s expenditures without authority from the Sanggunian and record all obligations during the fiscal year as obligations of that year.
We recommended that management discontinue the practice of piecemeal procurement. Consolidate the requirements of all departments and include the same in the annual procurement plan.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) enjoin the barangay officials to establish their own MRF; (b) exert effort to finalize the revise 10-year SWMP and submit to EMB for review and approval; and (c) ensure immediate compliance with the requirements imposed by the National Solid Waste Management Commission within the reglementary period.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant submit the financial reports, supporting documents and other schedules to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by existing rules and regulations to evade any disciplinary action that may be instituted against them.
9.7   We also recommended that the LCE augment the manpower needed by the accounting office to properly distribute the workload and help the Municipal Accountant expedite the submission of the financial reports, supporting documents and other schedules.

We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) LCE ensure strict implementation of the ordinances issued for the operations and franchising of tricycles and pedicab for hire especially on proper checking and inspection of the tricycles for the minimum requirements prior to the issuance or renewal of the franchise; and (b) SB members revisit/review the municipal  ordinances already enacted for the franchising/operations of tricycles/pedicab units and issue other ordinances, if necessary, in compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2007-01.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accountant, in coordination with other Offices involved to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies, and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing and payment.
We recommended that Management comply with the observations communicated through audit observation memoranda.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) BAC Chairman to ensure strict compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR; (b) Engineering Department prepare and submit PERT/CPM analysis and other required technical documents for every project implemented by the LGU; and (c) Accountant ensure that all payments for procurement of infrastructure projects bear the supporting documents such as project logbook as required by engineering requirements and standards of DPWH DO No. 216 series of 2000.
observations.reason:
Not all documents were completed.

Reiterated in Obs. No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Accountant, in coordination with other Offices involved to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies, ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing and payment, and refrain from charging prior year’s expenditures to current year’s appropriation without the authority from the Sanggunian Bayan; and (b) Municipal Treasurer ensure that payments made for expenses are duly acknowledged with official receipts/RER.
observations.reason:
Other deficiencies were not complied.

Reiterated in Obs. No. 9

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign a knowledgeable personnel who will cause the compilation of sufficient data to be incorporated in the 10-year solid waste management plan in accordance with RA No. 9003. We also recommended that the Local Solid Waste Management Board prepare a comprehensive work plan for its solid waste management, indicate the budget per activity and see to it that such plan works towards the Act’s objectives.
observations.reason:
Formulated the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan and was presented in the clarificatory hearing. Due to error on the result of WACs, the LGU will repeat the WACs.

Reiterated in Obs. No. 6
